metadata.title: Web Development Fundamentals
metadata.description: Comprehensive assessment covering HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and React basics

question.title: What is React?
question.description: Choose the correct answer about React

options.option: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces

question.explanation: React is a JavaScript library developed by Facebook for building user interfaces, particularly single-page applications.

question.title: HTTP Status Codes
question.description: Which HTTP status code indicates a successful request?
question.explanation: HTTP 200 OK indicates that the request has succeeded. The information returned with the response is dependent on the method used in the request.

question.title: CSS Flexbox Property
question.description: Which CSS property is used to make a flex container?
question.explanation: The display: flex; property is used to create a flex container, enabling flexbox layout for its children.

question.title: JavaScript Closures
question.description: What is a closure in JavaScript?

options.option: A function that has access to variables in its outer scope
A way to close browser windows
A method to end a loop
A syntax for closing HTML tags

question.explanation: A closure is a function that has access to variables in its outer (enclosing) lexical scope, even after the outer function has returned.

question.title: React useState Hook
question.description: What does the useState hook return?

options.option: An array with the current state and a function to update it
An object with state and setState properties

question.explanation: useState returns an array with two elements: the current state value and a function to update that state.

question.title: HTML5 Semantic Elements
question.description: Which of the following is NOT a semantic HTML5 element?
question.explanation: The <div> element is a generic container and not a semantic element. Semantic elements like <header>, <nav>, and <article> clearly describe their meaning to both the browser and the developer.

question.title: JavaScript Promises
question.description: What are the three states of a JavaScript Promise?
question.explanation: A Promise has three states: Pending (initial state), Fulfilled (operation completed successfully), and Rejected (operation failed).

question.title: CSS Grid Layout
question.description: Which property defines the number of columns in a CSS Grid?
question.explanation: The grid-template-columns property defines the number and size of columns in a CSS Grid layout.

question.title: React useEffect Hook
question.description: When does useEffect run by default?
question.explanation: By default, useEffect runs after every render (both mount and update). You can control this behavior using the dependency array.

question.title: ES6 Arrow Functions
question.description: What is the main difference between arrow functions and regular functions?

options.option: Arrow functions do not have their own 'this' binding

question.explanation: Arrow functions do not have their own 'this' context. They inherit 'this' from the enclosing lexical scope, which is often the desired behavior in callbacks and event handlers.
